commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Manual review is needed because the blocker is a product/security decision about repeated pre-approval code disclosure, not a safe automated repair.; Needs attention: The diff has no supply-chain concern, but it changes the DM pairing security boundary by repeatedly disclosing pending codes before approval.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve creation semantics when resending pending codes — src/pairing/pairing-challenge... (sha=c66d0a18ceb88755033f0ba35e0512758d91f7ea)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes shared DM pairing challenge issuance to resend a stored pending pairing code when an existing request still has a code.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR behavior delta: source inspection shows `{ code, created: false }` would fall through after the patch, and the inspected Telegram screenshot shows repeated `/start` replies. This is not a current-main bug reproduction because current docs and tests define the no-resend behavior.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the blocker is a product/security decision about repeated pre-approval code disclosure, not a safe automated repair.

target.title: Unapproved users receive pairing codes on every /start message before approval.
target.body: Unapproved users receive pairing codes on every /start message before approval. ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: Unapproved users receive pairing codes on every /start message before approval. Use when users need to repeatedly access pairing codes after the initial request, ensuring consistent access to pairing instructions even if the initial code was missed or lost. - Why it matters: - What changed: ``` if (created) { return { created: false }; } ``` changed to ``` if (!code) { return { created: false }; } ``` - What did NOT change (scope boundary): ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # ## User-visible / Behavior Changes List user-visible changes (including defaults/config). If none, write `None`. Every start message has response. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: - Runtime/container: - Model/provider: - Integration/channel (if any): - Relevant config (redacted): ### Steps 1. 2. 3. ### Expected - ### Actual - ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: - Edge cases checked: - What you did **not** verify: ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) - Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) - Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: - Files/config to restore: - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: - Mitigation:
